purpose: IF-4 intent/decision contradictions — ADR contradicted by code, stale workaround, name-lie + load-bearing TODO; and one legitimately-evolved BR that must NOT over-flag as DRIFTED

expected_issues.claim: possible decision contradiction: placeOrder dispatches the order-confirmation notification synchronously inline on the request path, which appears to contradict ADR-001 (notifications MUST be enqueued via NotificationQueue.enqueue() and the request MUST NOT block on a provider)
expected_issues.evidence: ADR-001 at .ai/docs/decisions.md:4 (Decision at :12 — 'MUST enqueue a job and return immediately ... MUST NOT call any notification provider synchronously inline') vs the direct `await sendEmail(...)` at src/orders/order.service.ts:28; exact contrast: the sibling cancelOrder path correctly uses NotificationQueue.enqueue() at src/orders/order.service.ts:46

expected_issues.claim: possible stale workaround WA-001: loadConfig still reads the config file twice and merges to defend against a partial-write race, but the original triggering condition appears no longer to hold
expected_issues.evidence: WA-001 recorded at .ai/docs/decisions.md:46 — triggering condition was the non-atomic write bug in @acme/atomic-writer@1.x; the import at src/config/loader.ts:2 now pins @2.0.1 ('atomic write fixed'), so the double-read at src/config/loader.ts:19 defends against a condition that no longer holds

expected_issues.claim: possible name-lie: saveConfigSync is named (and commented) as a synchronous write but is declared async and awaits an async writer, so a caller relying on the name will not have a completed write when the call returns
expected_issues.evidence: the identifier `saveConfigSync` and the inline comment 'Writes the config synchronously to disk.' contradict the `async ... Promise<void>` signature and `await writeFileAtomic(...)` at src/config/loader.ts:28-30

expected_issues.claim: possible load-bearing TODO on a Core path: order-ID generation is a TODO-flagged placeholder (Math.random) on the order-placement path, with the comment itself stating a collision silently merges two customers' orders
expected_issues.evidence: TODO at src/orders/order.service.ts:52 inside cryptoRandomId(), the ID source used by placeOrder() (Core order path); the comment marks it load-bearing ('Order IDs are the primary key used by the payment reconciliation job ... do not ship as-is')

must_not_fire.reason: BR-pricing:001's recorded intent is 'a discount reduces what the customer pays' (decisions.md:37). computeOrderTotal (src/orders/pricing.ts:15) has legitimately EVOLVED from a flat 10% to volume tiers + a stacking coupon (src/orders/pricing.ts:32), but every branch still reduces the payable total (clamped 0..subtotal). A naive detector would flag this as ADR/BR DRIFTED because the code diverged in style from the recorded rule; the IF-4(a) BR-specific drift definition requires the code to CONTRADICT the rule, not merely evolve — intent is preserved, so it MUST NOT fire.
